terface Receiver All Known Subinterfaces: ProcessReceiver All Known Implementing Classes: AbstractReceiver , CSPReceiver , DDEReceiver , MailboxBoundaryReceiver , PNQueueReceiver , RendezvousReceiver , RTPReceiver public interface Receiver Interface

, RTPReceiver public interface Receiver Interface for objects that can hold tokens. An implementation of this interface has two key methods: put() and get(). The put() method deposits a token into the receiver. The get() method retrieves a token t

he receiver. The get() method retrieves a token that has been put. The order of the retrieved tokens depends on specific implementations, and does not necessarily match the order in which tokens have been put. All implementations of this interface

ve been put. All implementations of this interface must follow these rules, regardless of the number of threads that are accessing the receiver: If hasToken() returns true, then the next call to get() must not result in a NoTokenException being thr

) must not result in a NoTokenException being thrown. If hasRoom() returns true, then the next call to put() must not result in a NoRoomException being thrown. In general, this means that multithreaded domains must provide synchronization for rece

ded domains must provide synchronization for receivers. Note that both NoTokenException and NoRoomException are runtime exceptions, so they need not be declared explicitly. Objects that implement this interface can only be contained by an instanc

his interface can only be contained by an instance of IOPort. Since: Ptolemy II 0.2 Version: $Id: Receiver.java,v 1.66 2006/06/17 14:27:40 eal Exp $ Author: Jie Liu, Edward A. Lee, Lukito Muliadi See Also: Token Accepted Rating: Green (bart) Propose

tionException - If clear() is not supported by the domain. get public Token get () throws NoTokenException Get a token from this receiver. Returns: A token read from the receiver. Throws: NoTokenException - If there is no token. getArray

argument specifies the number of tokens to get. In an implementation, the length of the returned array must be equal to numberOfTokens . Parameters: numberOfTokens - The number of tokens to get in the returned array. Returns: An array of tokens

okens - The number of tokens desired. Returns: True if the next numberOfTokens calls to get() will not result in a NoTokenException. isKnown public boolean isKnown () Return true if this receiver has known state; that is, the tokens in this rece

as known state; that is, the tokens in this receiver are known, or this receiver is known not to contain any tokens. This method supports domains, such as SR, which have fixed-point semantics. In such domains, an iteration of a model starts

In such domains, an iteration of a model starts with the state of all channels unknown, and the iteration concludes when the state of all channels is known. In domains that have no such notion, this method should simply return true . Returns:

, this method should simply return true . Returns: True if this receiver has known state. put public void put ( Token token) throws NoRoomException , IllegalActionException Put the specified token into this receiver. Parameters: token - The

token into this receiver. Parameters: token - The token to put into the receiver. Throws: NoRoomException - If there is no room in the receiver. IllegalActionException - If the token is not acceptable to one of the ports (e.g., wrong type). putArr
